title: Put the malware removal process in order
instructions: A workstation shows signs of malware infection. Number the remediation steps in the correct CompTIA order.
steps: Identify and research malware symptoms
Quarantine the infected system
Disable System Restore in Windows
Remediate infected systems
Schedule scans and run updates
Enable System Restore and create a restore point
Educate the end user
note: CompTIA expects the malware removal sequence: identify, quarantine, disable restore, remediate, schedule scans/updates, re-enable restore, educate.

title: Match Windows repair commands to the correct task
instructions: Match each support task to the command that best fits.

items.prompt: Repair protected Windows system files
Repair the Windows component store used by SFC
Check and repair file-system errors on the next reboot if needed
Rebuild boot configuration data after boot failure
Refresh domain Group Policy settings

note: SFC repairs system files, DISM repairs the component store, CHKDSK repairs file-system errors, Bootrec repairs boot records/BCD, and GPUpdate refreshes policy.

title: Match network symptoms to commands
instructions: Choose the best first command for each troubleshooting task.

items.prompt: A PC can ping 8.8.8.8 but cannot resolve google.com
A stale DNS record is cached locally
The PC has a 169.254.x.x APIPA address
You need to see the default gateway, DNS servers, and DHCP status
You need to see the path packets take to a remote host

note: Match the command to the evidence. DNS resolution, DHCP lease problems, and route/path testing use different tools.

title: Match security technologies to protections
instructions: Match each technology to what it primarily protects.

items.prompt: Protects an entire Windows volume if the drive is removed
Encrypts individual files and folders for a user
Stores cryptographic keys and supports measured boot
Verifies trusted bootloaders before OS startup
Controls file access while the OS enforces permissions

note: BitLocker is full-volume encryption. EFS is file/folder encryption. Secure Boot is boot integrity, not data encryption.

title: Match backup types to behavior
instructions: Match the backup type to its behavior.

items.prompt: Backs up everything selected every time
Backs up changes since the last full backup
Backs up changes since the last backup of any type
Point-in-time copy often used before risky changes
Proves that backups are usable before a disaster

note: Differential grows until the next full backup. Incremental chains depend on the previous backup set.

title: Order a safe change-management workflow
instructions: A technician needs to deploy a firewall configuration change. Put the steps in the safest order.
steps: Document the proposed change and business reason
Assess risk and affected systems
Get approval from the proper authority or CAB
Schedule the maintenance window and notify stakeholders
Back up the current configuration and prepare a rollback plan
Implement the approved change
Validate the change and monitor for issues
Document the result and lessons learned
note: Change management protects production systems by requiring planning, approval, rollback, validation, and documentation.

title: Order printer troubleshooting steps
instructions: A user cannot print to a network printer. Put these actions in the best troubleshooting order.
steps: Identify whether one user or multiple users are affected
Verify the printer is powered on and online
Check network connectivity to the printer
Check the print queue for stuck jobs
Restart the print spooler if jobs are stuck
Update or reinstall the printer driver if needed
Print a test page and document the fix
note: Start by scoping the issue, then verify simple causes before changing drivers or services.

title: Match mobile symptoms to likely fixes
instructions: Match each mobile support scenario to the best first action.

items.prompt: New cellular provider; mobile data does not work
Work email stopped syncing after password change
Battery drains quickly after installing a new app
Phone will not pair with car audio
User needs mail synchronized across phone, tablet, and laptop

note: Mobile questions often hinge on the clue: APN for carrier data, credentials for sync, app usage for battery, pairing for Bluetooth.

title: Match Linux commands to tasks
instructions: Match each Linux task to the most appropriate command.

items.prompt: Show the current working directory
List files, including hidden files, in long format
Change file permissions
Change file ownership
Search a text stream for matching lines
Show running processes

note: Core 2 expects basic Linux command recognition, especially file navigation, permissions, and process viewing.

title: Match permission requirements to best control
instructions: Choose the best permission/control for each requirement.

items.prompt: Allow a user to read and run a program but not modify it
Allow a department to add files but not change NTFS ownership
Allow administrators to change permissions and ownership
Apply access based on group membership instead of individual users
Preserve least privilege when a user moves departments

note: CompTIA favors least privilege and group-based access over direct user-by-user permissions.

title: Match social engineering attacks
instructions: Match the attack description to the correct term.

items.prompt: A text message asks the user to verify a bank login
A phone caller impersonates IT support
A targeted email is sent to the CFO
An attacker follows an employee through a secured door
An attacker watches a user type a PIN
An attacker searches discarded paperwork for information

note: Know the communication channel and target: SMS is smishing, voice is vishing, executives are whaling.

title: Match Windows tools to tasks
instructions: Match the administrative task to the Windows tool.

items.prompt: View CPU, memory, disk, and startup app usage
Manage local users, disks, services, and Event Viewer from one console
View driver status and roll back a driver
View system and application logs
Create or shrink disk partitions
Schedule scripts or maintenance jobs

note: Windows tool PBQs are often scenario-based: pick the utility that directly exposes the setting or evidence.

title: Ticket simulator: slow Windows PC
instructions: Work the ticket by choosing the best action at each step. Use least-invasive troubleshooting.

stages.prompt: Ticket: User reports the PC is extremely slow after login. What do you do first?
options: Open Task Manager and check resource usage
stages.answer: Open Task Manager and check resource usage
stages.result: Task Manager shows Disk at 100%, CPU at 8%, Memory at 45%.
A backup client is constantly retrying a failed cloud sync job.

stages.prompt: What is the best next action?
options: Pause/fix the backup client and verify performance
stages.answer: Pause/fix the backup client and verify performance
stages.result: Disk usage drops and applications open normally.

stages.prompt: What should you do before closing the ticket?
options: Document symptoms, cause, fix, and verification
Tell the user to reboot weekly only
stages.answer: Document symptoms, cause, fix, and verification

note: The scenario tests evidence gathering, resource bottleneck analysis, least-invasive action, verification, and documentation.

title: Ticket simulator: browser redirects and pop-ups
instructions: Work the malware-style ticket using the best next action.

stages.prompt: User reports homepage changes, redirects, and pop-ups. What is the most likely issue?
stages.result: The browser has unknown extensions installed.

options: Quarantine the system and prevent spread
Join it to a new domain
stages.answer: Quarantine the system and prevent spread
stages.result: The system is isolated from the network.

options: Remove suspicious extensions and run updated anti-malware scans
stages.answer: Remove suspicious extensions and run updated anti-malware scans
stages.result: Scans remove unwanted software and browser settings are reset.

options: Educate the user about suspicious downloads/extensions
Give the user local admin permanently
stages.answer: Educate the user about suspicious downloads/extensions

note: This blends browser hijacker symptoms with the malware removal process.

title: Ticket simulator: secure website certificate error
instructions: Choose the best step for each stage.

stages.prompt: User can browse most sites, but a secure site says 'Your connection is not private.' What do you check first?
stages.result: The laptop date is set two years in the past.

options: Correct date/time and time zone, then retry
stages.answer: Correct date/time and time zone, then retry
stages.result: The secure site now loads without certificate errors.

options: Symptom, incorrect clock, correction, and verification
stages.answer: Symptom, incorrect clock, correction, and verification
stages.result: The ticket has useful closure notes.

note: Incorrect system time can make valid certificates appear expired or not yet valid.

title: Ticket simulator: domain password reset
instructions: Choose the best action in a domain-joined environment.

stages.prompt: A user forgot their password on a domain-joined Windows laptop. What should you use?
options: Active Directory Users and Computers or approved identity tool
stages.answer: Active Directory Users and Computers or approved identity tool
stages.result: The user account is found in AD.

stages.prompt: What should you do before resetting?
options: Verify the user's identity according to policy
Ask the user for their old password
stages.answer: Verify the user's identity according to policy

stages.prompt: After reset, what should the user be required to do?
options: Store password in a text file
stages.result: The user signs in and sets a new password.

note: Domain accounts are managed centrally. Always verify identity before password resets.

title: Command simulator: flush a corrupted DNS cache
instructions: Type the Windows command that clears the local DNS resolver cache.
note: Use ipconfig /flushdns when a stale or corrupted local DNS cache is suspected.

title: Command simulator: repair Windows system files
instructions: Type the command that scans and repairs protected Windows system files.
note: SFC repairs protected Windows system files.

title: Command simulator: repair the Windows component store
instructions: SFC cannot repair files because the component store is damaged. Type the DISM command to repair it.
note: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth repairs the online Windows image/component store.

title: Command simulator: force Group Policy update
instructions: Type the command that forces a Windows computer to refresh Group Policy.
note: gpupdate /force refreshes local and domain Group Policy settings.

title: Order incident response actions
instructions: A user opened a suspicious attachment and ransomware activity is suspected. Put the actions in the best order.
steps: Disconnect the system from the network
Escalate according to incident response policy
Preserve evidence and document observed symptoms
Identify affected systems and scope
Contain the threat
Eradicate malware and remove persistence
Recover from known-good backups
Review lessons learned and update controls
note: Containment and escalation happen before broad remediation. Do not destroy evidence by jumping straight to reinstalling.

title: Match software licensing models
instructions: Match each licensing model to its description.

items.prompt: License tied to original hardware/manufacturer installation
Purchased by an individual or small organization, transferable depending on terms
Used by organizations to manage many licenses centrally
Source code is available under license terms
Access continues while recurring payment remains active

note: Core 2 tests licensing at a practical technician level: know the model and follow the license terms.

title: Match safety concerns to best action
instructions: Match each safety situation to the best response.

items.prompt: Working inside a desktop PC
Swollen laptop battery
items.answer: Stop using it and follow battery disposal/escalation procedures

items.prompt: Printer toner spill
items.answer: Use proper cleanup method and PPE; do not use a regular vacuum

items.prompt: Damaged power cable
items.answer: Remove from service and replace it

items.prompt: Heavy equipment must be moved
items.answer: Use proper lifting technique or get assistance

note: Safety questions usually reward avoiding harm before continuing technical work.

title: Match Windows recovery options
instructions: Match each problem to the least-invasive recovery option.

items.prompt: Bad display driver after update; Windows can boot into Safe Mode
items.answer: Roll back or uninstall the driver in Safe Mode

items.prompt: Recent application install broke Windows features; restore point exists
Windows will not boot and startup files are damaged
User needs Windows reinstalled while preserving personal files
items.answer: Reset this PC - Keep my files

items.prompt: Drive has failed SMART diagnostics
items.answer: Replace the drive and restore data

note: Always pick the least-invasive option that addresses the evidence.

title: Order the troubleshooting methodology
instructions: Put the standard troubleshooting process in order.
steps: Identify the problem
Establish a theory of probable cause
Test the theory to determine cause
Establish a plan of action and implement the solution
Verify full system functionality and implement preventive measures
Document findings, actions, and outcomes
note: This methodology applies across Windows, mobile, malware, networking, and operational scenarios.
